description: Happy path: Microsoft profiles: tenant-owned named Microsoft profiles exist as the shared credential model for Teams.

Tenant migration creates a default Microsoft profile, preserves existing Microsoft credentials, and leaves legacy email/calendar/MSP SSO consumers working.
Tenant admin can create multiple named Microsoft profiles, mark one default, and bind a different selected profile specifically to Teams.
Tenant admin sees Teams setup blocked with clear remediation when no eligible Microsoft profile exists.
Tenant-specific Teams package metadata uses the selected Microsoft profile identity and correct environment URLs.
MSP technician can open the Teams personal tab via SSO without a second PSA sign-in prompt and land on the my-work view.
Activity-feed deep link opens the intended ticket inside the Teams tab for an authorized MSP user.
Bot command `my tickets` returns authorized ticket results with action links for the signed-in MSP technician.
Bot command `ticket <id>` returns a summary for an authorized ticket and a not-found response for an invalid ID.
Bot command `assign ticket` updates the assignee through the shared Teams action layer and confirms the result.
Bot command `add note` appends an internal note to the target ticket and returns a follow-up deep link.
Bot command `reply to contact` creates a customer-facing reply when permitted and blocks the action when not permitted.
Bot command `log time` creates a time entry against a ticket or task using the quick-action form flow.
Bot approvals command lists pending approvals and completes approve/reject actions through the shared action layer.
Message extension search returns tenant-scoped ticket, task, contact, and approval matches only when the invoking user has permission.
Message action `create ticket from message` creates a ticket with Teams source metadata and returns a deep link.
Message action `update from message` appends an internal note to an existing ticket with Teams source metadata and returns a deep link.
Shared quick-action dialogs validate required fields, submit through the shared action layer, and show success/failure responses in Teams.
Assignment, customer-reply, approval-request, escalation, and SLA-risk events each produce personal Teams notifications when enabled.
Teams personal notifications are suppressed safely when the tenant category is disabled or the user lacks Teams linkage.
Unauthorized, wrong-tenant, or client-portal users are rejected across tab, bot, and message extension entry points.
